title: Santo Domingo
content:
In the crypt with Columbus
        in the crypt with Giovanni
        of Genoa, the diaspora driven Jew;
        watching flecks of the cathedral floor
        jade-eyed and mica afraid
        yawning down brown the abyss, his skeletal coffin
        thin accae wood,
        phlegm coloured
        flamed ointment
        of the saints
        in holy water
        bridging the little centuries.

        2
        Serpentine heavens
        in coiled stars
        heaving like passion fruit
        hung down piano wire.

        3
        Meteors douse the light
        of black stems,
        eye holes cut of old Spanish
        sailors; thin ghosts
        plundering night.

        4
        Melange tableaux
        peut-�tre les �toiles
        sont oiseaux.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Paul Cameron Brown, titled "Santo Domingo", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
